title: EB infra substrate hygiene — close 5 gap classes + VPC peering capture
description: Closes the five gap classes surfaced by Cat 3 coverage suite at 2026-06-03 substrate read: (1) cis-services-1 grant+posture FK targets absent from catalog; (2) ectropy-terraform-state bucket missing from env-prod.objectStorage; (3) ectropy-development-configs missing from env-dev.objectStorage; (4) two credentialStore items lack rotation policy; (5) credentialStore items lack livenessProbe. Also captures live VPC peering 768a1eb6 in catalog.vpcPeerings[] per memory #20 (enterprise-complete when v1.2.0 vpcPeerings landed today specifically to enable drift-vpcpeering). EB-only scope; no schema changes; both instances stay at current version with auditTrail entries appended.

outputs.description: Add server entry server-cis-services-1 to env-cis.servers[] in infrastructure-catalog.json. Schema env-cis already exists in environments[]; this populates its servers[] (currently empty/absent). Server entry shape: id=server-cis-services-1, name=cis-services-1, provider=digitalocean, region=sfo3, ip=143.110.238.160, services=[]. Resolves the FK target for resource-access.json ag-cis-services-1.resourceRef and hostPosture[].serverRef. sshConfig deliberately omitted (host is zero-ssh per resource-access.json hostPosture; an sshConfig on the catalog entry would contradict the access graph). specs omitted — not in current substrate. services[] empty — CIS services live in architecture.json per memory #12 dual-home pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: infrastructure-catalog.json env-cis.servers[] contains exactly one entry with id=server-cis-services-1
server entry ip=143.110.238.160 verbatim (doctl-verified 2026-05-23)
server entry provider=digitalocean, region=sfo3
ajv-cli validates infrastructure-catalog.json against schemas.luh.tech/infrastructure-catalog.schema.json v1.2.0 with exit 0

outputs.description: Update resource-access.json to point ag-cis-services-1.resourceRef and hostPosture[0].serverRef from the bare hostname 'cis-services-1' to the canonical catalog id 'server-cis-services-1'. Closes the dangling-resourceRefs finding (coverage-infrastructure surfaced grantNotInCatalog + postureNotInCatalog at 2026-06-03 substrate read).
acceptanceCriteria: resource-access.json accessGrants[] entry with id=ag-cis-services-1 has resourceRef=server-cis-services-1
resource-access.json hostPosture[] entry has serverRef=server-cis-services-1
eb-agent dangling-resourceRefs returns 0 dangling entries
eb-agent coverage-infrastructure returns 0 grantNotInCatalog and 0 postureNotInCatalog

outputs.description: Add objectStorage entry to env-prod.objectStorage[] for the Terraform state backend bucket. Entry shape: purpose=terraform-state, provider=do-spaces, region=sfo3, endpoint=https://sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com, bucket=ectropy-terraform-state. Closes catalogNotCovered for ectropy-terraform-state and resolves FU-EB-CATALOG-TF-STATE-BUCKET-MISSING-1.
acceptanceCriteria: infrastructure-catalog.json env-prod.objectStorage[] contains an entry with bucket=ectropy-terraform-state, purpose=terraform-state

FU-EB-CATALOG-TF-STATE-BUCKET-MISSING-1 status transitions open -> closed in follow-up-register (scope of THIS deliverable is hygiene fills only; FU closure happens via separate micro-PR or is folded into this PR if scope allows — see notes)

outputs.description: Add objectStorage entry to env-dev.objectStorage[] for the development config-sync bucket. env-dev currently has no objectStorage field; this creates it with one entry. Entry shape: purpose=config-sync, provider=do-spaces, region=sfo3, endpoint=https://sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com, bucket=ectropy-development-configs. Closes catalogNotCovered for ectropy-development-configs and resolves FU-EB-CATALOG-DEV-OBJECTSTORAGE-POPULATE-1.
acceptanceCriteria: infrastructure-catalog.json env-dev.objectStorage[] exists and contains exactly one entry with bucket=ectropy-development-configs, purpose=config-sync
FU-EB-CATALOG-DEV-OBJECTSTORAGE-POPULATE-1 status transitions open -> closed in follow-up-register (per OUT-3 scope note)

outputs.description: Add rotation block and livenessProbe block to both credentialStore.items[] entries in resource-access.json (TF_DO_SPACES_STAGING itemId 2cffb9fb-9123-4bb0-b244-b3e7000fe2af, TF_DO_SPACES_PROD itemId f89b9344-3542-43ae-acbf-b3e7002d1904). Rotation policy=on-exposure for both, lastRotated=null, trackedByFollowUp=null. livenessProbe for 2cffb9fb=not-tested with nulls (no probe evidence in substrate). livenessProbe for f89b9344=success with lastVerifiedReachable=2026-06-02T20:17:39Z and verifiedBy=d-2026-06-02-prod-spaces-credentials-graph (real probe data from STEP 1-PRE-D bucket access matrix that produced the 2026-06-02 OUT-1 commit, per resource-access.json auditTrail entry).
acceptanceCriteria: credentialStore.items[] entry for 2cffb9fb has rotation.policy=on-exposure, rotation.lastRotated=null, rotation.trackedByFollowUp=null
credentialStore.items[] entry for f89b9344 has rotation.policy=on-exposure, rotation.lastRotated=null, rotation.trackedByFollowUp=null
credentialStore.items[] entry for 2cffb9fb has livenessProbe.lastVerifiedResult=not-tested, livenessProbe.lastVerifiedReachable=null, livenessProbe.verifiedBy=null

credentialStore.items[] entry for f89b9344 has livenessProbe.lastVerifiedResult=success, livenessProbe.lastVerifiedReachable=2026-06-02T20:17:39Z, livenessProbe.verifiedBy=d-2026-06-02-prod-spaces-credentials-graph
eb-agent drift-rotation returns 0 noPolicy entries
eb-agent drift-liveness returns 0 drifted entries (success and not-tested are non-drift states)

outputs.description: Capture live VPC peering 768a1eb6 in catalog.vpcPeerings[]. Field landed in schema v1.2.0 today (PR #106, SR main 98534ab) specifically to enable drift-vpcpeering. Entry shape: id=vpc-peering-768a1eb6, doId=768a1eb6, envA=env-staging, envB=env-prod, region=sfo3, status=pending-elimination, trackedByFollowUp=null (no existing FU tracks elimination — operator memory flags it; if an FU is needed, file separately under hygiene followup), notes=staging<->production VPC peering, flagged for elimination per multi-session memory.
acceptanceCriteria: infrastructure-catalog.json catalog.vpcPeerings[] contains one entry with id=vpc-peering-768a1eb6, doId=768a1eb6, status=pending-elimination
envA and envB FK into existing environments[] (env-staging, env-prod both present at substrate read)
eb-agent drift-vpcpeering returns 1 pendingElimination entry (vpc-peering-768a1eb6)

preMerge: eb-agent coverage-infrastructure: catalogNotCovered drops by 2 (ectropy-terraform-state, ectropy-development-configs); grantNotInCatalog=0; postureNotInCatalog=0
git diff --stat scoped to exactly .roadmap/infrastructure-catalog.json and .roadmap/resource-access.json — no other file touched
PR CI green; --admin merge forbidden per memory #15
postMerge: eb-agent coverage-infrastructure on main reflects the closure (catalogNotCovered: 11 services/dbs remain as expected, 2 buckets closed)
Cat 3b tools (drift-liveness, drift-vpcpeering) return real-data results on main

transitions.trigger: spec authored against canonical deliverable.schema.json v1.1.0 per F0B85LNSTC4 doctrine

notes: Scope decision: this deliverable closes the 5 gap classes named at 2026-06-03 substrate read PLUS the VPC peering capture (per memory #20, enterprise-complete when v1.2.0 vpcPeerings landed today specifically to make drift-vpcpeering queryable). The 11 services+DBs in coverage-infrastructure catalogNotCovered are deliberately deferred — they require substrate-decision work on whether ops grants are the right modeling vs env-routes-only reachability, not hygiene-fill. FU closure for OUT-3/OUT-4 references FU-EB-CATALOG-TF-STATE-BUCKET-MISSING-1 and FU-EB-CATALOG-DEV-OBJECTSTORAGE-POPULATE-1 from the 2026-06-02 field coverage assessment; closure can ship in-scope by adding follow-up-register.json to scopeFiles (would expand to 3 files) OR via separate micro-PR after merge. Current spec keeps scopeFiles tight to 2 to preserve branch scope-lock per memory #18 — FU closure micro-PR follows as a planned succession. Cross-repo: this deliverable is EB-only execution but the spec instance must land in luh-tech/schema-registry under schemas/_instances/deliverables/ per the proven cross-repo pattern (2026-06-03 today). SR-chat lands instance via micro-PR; EB-chat executes the data fills; SR closes with TRUTH-DELIVERED. Per memory #14 the resource-access access graph is FK-one-direction into infrastructure-catalog — OUT-2 updates the FK target (resourceRef) only after OUT-1 lands the catalog entry; both happen in the same PR so atomicity holds. Spelling Luhtala verified per memory #22.
